does: This paper introduces DuET-PD, a comprehensive evaluation framework that measures how Large Language Models respond to persuasive dialogue in multi-turn interactions across two critical dimensions: the type of persuasion (corrective guidance or misleading statements) and the domain (factual knowledge via MMLU-Pro or safety via SALAD-Bench). The authors conduct extensive testing on state-of-the-art models including GPT-4o and find that even leading models achieve poor performance under sustained misleading persuasion, with GPT-4o reaching only 27.32% accuracy on knowledge tasks. To address these vulnerabilities, they propose Holistic DPO, a novel training method that balances positive and negative persuasion examples to simultaneously improve both robustness against misinformation and receptiveness to valid corrections. When applied to Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, Holistic DPO dramatically improves performance on safety tasks under misleading persuasion from 4.21% to 76.54%.
connects: This work extends the growing body of research examining LLM reliability and alignment in adversarial settings, building on prior investigations into model vulnerability to prompt injection, jailbreaking, and adversarial inputs. It responds to recent findings showing that LLMs exhibit problematic sycophancy and difficulty distinguishing between helpful feedback and harmful misinformation in conversational contexts. The paper builds on existing evaluation methodologies like MMLU and SALAD-Bench while introducing a novel dual-dimension framework for systematic assessment. Holistic DPO advances beyond earlier approaches like standard DPO (Direct Preference Optimization) and resist-only training by recognizing that robustness and adaptability require balanced training signals rather than defensive filtering alone, connecting to broader work on learning from diverse preference signals.
enables: This research opens pathways toward developing more trustworthy LLMs capable of navigating real-world deployment where models encounter mixed streams of corrective feedback and misleading information. The DuET-PD framework provides a foundation for broader evaluation of persuasion resistance across diverse domains and model architectures, enabling systematic benchmarking of progress. Holistic DPO's success suggests that training methods balancing opposing signals could address the core tension between gullibility and inflexibility, potentially guiding future work in alignment beyond just safety to include reliability in knowledge-seeking contexts. The findings motivate investigation of curriculum-based training, mixture-of-experts approaches, and uncertainty calibration to further strengthen model judgment under persuasive pressure.
